10. Pric les  institutions  specialisees  et  les  autres organismes competents des Nations U nies de coope› rer etroitement avec le  Secretaire general afin de faci› liter le  versement de contributions pour le  Botswana;
items_en: 7. Insta111mc11t the Member States and bodies which already implement or negotiate assistance programmes for Botswana to extend them whenever possible;

8. the United Nations Development Programme, the United Nations Children's Fund. the World Food Programme,!'Orga› the World Health Organization, the World Bank and the International Fund for Agricultural Development to draw the attention of their governing bodies to their assistance to Botswana, for which the General Assembly requested the Secretary-General to carry out a special programme of economic assistance, and to report on the results of their assistance and decisions to the Secretary-General in time for consideration by the General Assembly at its thirty-eighth session;
9. Launches 1111 appcl to the international community to contribute to the special account opened by the Secretary General in order to facilitate the payment of contributions for Botswana;
10. The specialized institutions and other competent bodies of the United Nations shall cooperate closely with the Secretary-General in order to facilitate the payment of contributions for Botswana;

items_fr: a) De  poursuivre  ses  efforts  en  vue  de  mobiliser les  ressources  necessaires a un  programme  efficace d'assistance  financiere.  technique  et  materielle  au Botswana;

items_en: (a) Continue its efforts to mobilize the necessary resources for an effective programme of technical and material financial assistance in Botswana;

(h) To keep the situation in Botswana under review, to keep in close contact with Member States, regional organizations and other intergovernmental organizations, specialised institutions and interested international financial institutions, and to report to the Economic and Social Council at its second ordinary session in I983 on the progress of the special economic assistance programme for Botswana;

(c) To report on the economic developments in Botswana and the progress made in the organization and implementation of the special economic assistance programme for Botswana, in time for the General Assembly to consider the matter at its thirty-eighth session.

text_en: Ayon/C.llllillc the report of the Secretary General 44 · to which is attached the report of the inter-agency mission which he sent to Liberia in March 1982 to consult the Government on the additional assistance required for the reconstitution.

text_fr: Nota11t que.  scion  le  rapport,  le  Liberia  se  heurte i1 de graves problemes economiques et financiers qui decoulent  essentiellement  de  la  faiblesse  et  du  sous› developpement  de  son  infrastructure  economique  et sociale.
text_en: The report notes that Liberia faces serious economic and financial problems, mainly due to the weakness and underdevelopment of its economic and social infrastructure.

text_fr: Nota11t  1;galc111c11t que.  selon  le  rapport,  la  situa› tion budgetaire du  Liberia met le  Gouvernement dans l'impossibilite d'entreprendre un programme de deve› loppement  s'il  ne  dispose  pas  d'une aide  financiere exterieure suffisante.
text_en: Note 11t 1;galc111c11t that, according to the report, the budgetary position of Liberia makes it impossible for the Government to undertake a development programme if it does not have sufficient external financial assistance.

text_fr: Sllchant que  le  Gouvernement  liberien  a  !'inten› tion  d'organiser en 1983, avec  le  concours  du  Pro› gramme  des  Nations  Unies  pour  le  developpement. une  table  ronde  de  donateurs  en  vue  d'examiner les besoins  de  developpement  du  pays  et  d'etudier les moyens d'appuyer les efforts que fait le Gouvernement pour y  satisfaire.
text_en: In 1983, with the assistance of the United Nations Development Programme, the Government of Liberia initiated a round table of donors to examine the country's development needs and to explore ways of supporting the Government's efforts to meet them.

text_en: Noting that the Government of Liberia, together with the Secretary of the United Nations, prepared and presented to the Development Planning Committee for consideration at its nineteenth session in 1983, a report containing additional information and updated on the economic situation in Liberia.

items_en: 4. Strongly renews/calls for all Member States to contribute, through bilateral or multi-lateral channels, to the reconstruction. to the raising and development of Liberia:

5. Urges Member States, United Nations programmes and agencies, regional and inter-regional government agencies, development and financing institutions and non-governmental organizations to fully support the efforts of the Government of Liberia to mobilize funds for its special programme of economic assistance and, to this end, to respond in a comprehensive manner to the needs of Liberia at the next round table;

6. Requests the relevant programmes and agencies of the United Nations system, in particular the United Nations Development Programme, the World Bank, the United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization, the International Fund for Agricultural Development, the World Food Programme, the World Health Organization, the United Nations Children's Fund and the United Nations Industrial Development Organization, to continue and increase their assistance programmes in Liberia, to cooperate closely with the Secretary-General in his efforts to organize an effective international programme of assistance and to report periodically to the Secretary-General on the measures they have taken and the resources they have made available to assist that country:

7. Calls on regional and inter-regional organisations and other inter-governmental bodies and non-governmental organisations, as well as international financing institutions - in particular the European Economic Community› peenne, the European Development Fund, the African Development Bank.

8. Calls upon Member States, pending consideration by the Committee on Development Planning at its nineteenth session of the report submitted to it and in view of the critical economic situation in Liberia, to provide for specific measures for Liberia and, as a matter of priority, to consider including it in their development assistance programmes in the near future:

9. Urges my Member States and the competent United Nations bodies in particular› to link the United Nations Development Programme› pement, the World Food Programme, the United Nations Children's Fund, the World Health Organization, the United Nations Population Fund and the United Nations Organization for Industrial Development› triel to provide the Government of Liberia with all possible assistance to help meet the critical humanitarian needs of the population and the humanitarian needs of the people.

items_en: to provide it, as appropriate, with food, medicines and the necessary school and hospital equipment, as well as to meet the urgent needs of the population living in the receiving region, as a result of the floods and floods:

10. the United Nations Development Programme. the United Nations Children's Fund, the World Food Programme, the World Health Organization, the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations. the World Bank and the International Fund for Agricultural Development to draw the attention of their governing bodies, for their consideration, to the particular needs of Liberia and to report on the decisions taken by those bodies to the Secretary-General before 15 July 1983:
11. Requests the Administrator of the United Nations Development Programme and the World Bank to provide all possible assistance to the Liberal Government in organizing the donor round table:
12. Requests the Secretary-General:
(a) Continue its efforts to mobilize the necessary resources for an effective programme of financial, technical and material assistance in Liberia:
(h) To ensure that the necessary financial and budgetary arrangements are made to continue to organize the international assistance programme for Liberia and to mobilize this assistance:

(c) To keep the situation concerning assistance to Liberia under constant review. to remain in close contact with the Member States. regional organizations and other intergovernmental organizations, specialized institutions and international financial institutions concerned. and to report to the Economic and Social Council at its second ordinary session in 1983.

(d) To report on the economic developments in Liberia and on the progress made in the organization and implementation of the assistance programme to Liberia, in time for the Assern› blee generale to consider the matter at its thirty-eighth session.
